627	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
628	# Hard restriction scopes (the enclosing spans that confer hardness)
629	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
630	
631	def hard_restriction_scopes(clauses: list[Clause]) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
632	    """Verbatim spans of every clause whose deontic cue is a HARD restriction /
633	    prohibition / obligation. A quality/date/coordination span enclosed by one of
634	    these inherits its hardness — the generalized "only"-scope rule."""
635	    scopes: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
636	    for c in clauses:
637	        if c.deontic is not None and c.deontic.family in ("restrict", "exclude", "oblige") \
638	                and c.deontic.force == FORCE_HARD:
639	            scopes.append((c.start, c.end))
640	    return scopes
641	
642	
643	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
644	# Ledger parsers driver — augment reconcile_candidates output
645	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
646	
647	def ledger_candidates(
648	    prompt: str, clauses: list[Clause], *, ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
649	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
650	    """Run every deterministic ledger parser and return the augmenting candidates.
651	
652	    Additive to ``reconcile_candidates``: these catch the constraints the champion
653	    intake extractor structurally misses (bare quality, unknown-kind exclusion,
654	    coordination, date-hardness inheritance). All hardness is scope-inherited from
655	    the clause segmentation — never invented.
656	
657	    Source-kind values are canonicalized against the same ontology alias index
658	    ``reconcile_candidates`` uses, and an UNMAPPED kind is marked ``opaque`` (a
659	    first-class value, honestly surfaced as blocked_unsupported rather than
660	    silently claimed enforceable) — mirroring the adapter's own opaque discipline.
661	    """
662	    scopes = hard_restriction_scopes(clauses)
663	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
664	    out.extend(parse_quality(prompt, hard_scopes=scopes))
665	    out.extend(parse_exclusions(prompt))
666	    out.extend(parse_coordination(prompt, hard_scopes=scopes))
667	    out.extend(parse_date_bound(prompt, hard_scopes=scopes))
668	
669	    # Ontology-canonicalize source-kind members; an unmapped kind stays verbatim
670	    # and is marked opaque (never dropped) — the same pass reconcile_candidates
671	    # applies, so a ledger member and an intake facet reconcile identically.
672	    alias_index = _ontology_source_type_index(ontology)
673	    if alias_index:
674	        for cand in out:
675	            if cand.dimension == "source.types":
676	                canon, mapped = _canonicalize_source_type(cand.value, alias_index)
677	                cand.value = canon
678	                if not mapped:
679	                    cand.normalization_status = NORM_OPAQUE
680	    return out
681	
682	
683	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
684	# Opaque preservation — a deontic clause no TERM covered becomes a term
685	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
686	
687	def opaque_terms_for_uncovered(
688	    prompt: str, clauses: list[Clause], contract: Any,
689	) -> list[ContractTerm]:
690	    """Author an OPAQUE :class:`ContractTerm` for every deontic-marked constraint
691	    clause that NO existing CONTRACT TERM span overlaps.
692	
693	    This is the lossless guarantee that does not depend on an extractor existing:
694	    a constraint-bearing clause the deterministic parsers could not normalize into
695	    a surviving term is preserved verbatim as ``normalization_status=opaque`` —
696	    force inherited from its deontic cue, raw clause text + span, ``stage_owner``
697	    best-guess. It is ``origin=explicit`` (a real span) so it is authoritative,
698	    but opaque so the enforcement state honestly becomes ``blocked_unsupported`` —
699	    never silence.
700	
701	    Coverage is keyed on the CONTRACT'S OWN TERMS (LLM + deterministic-authored),
702	    the SAME basis :func:`validate_completeness` uses. So a clause covered only by
703	    a candidate that never became a term (e.g. a deliverable candidate the merge
704	    path does not author) still gets an opaque term here, and completeness cannot
705	    then flag it — the two checks share one coverage definition (no phantom
706	    ``clause_undispositioned``).
707	    """
708	    # every contract-term span (opaque or normalized).
709	    term_spans: list[tuple[int, int]] = [
710	        (sp.start, sp.end) for term in contract.all_terms() for sp in term.spans
711	    ]
712	
713	    def _covered(clause: Clause) -> bool:
714	        for (s, e) in term_spans:
715	            if s < clause.end and e > clause.start:
716	                return True
717	        return False
718	
719	    terms: list[ContractTerm] = []
720	    n = 0
721	    for clause in clauses:
722	        if not clause.is_constraint_bearing():
723	            continue
724	        if _covered(clause):
725	            continue
726	        cue = clause.deontic
727	        assert cue is not None
728	        n += 1
729	        # subject/attribute best-guess from the cue family.
730	        subject = "source"
731	        stage_owner = "eligibility"
732	        if cue.family == "oblige":
733	            subject, stage_owner = "topic", "compose"
734	        term = ContractTerm(
735	            term_id=f"opaque.{clause.clause_id}",
736	            dimension="scope.opaque" if subject == "source" else "content.opaque",
737	            value=clause.text,
738	            origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT,
739	            force=cue.force,
740	            spans=[PromptSpan(clause.start, clause.end, clause.text)],
741	            subject=subject,
742	            attribute=cue.attribute or "constraint",
743	            operator=OP_NOT_IN if cue.family == "exclude" else OP_IN,
744	            value_set=[clause.text],
745	            stage_owner=stage_owner,
746	            normalization_status=NORM_OPAQUE,
747	            enforcement_stages=["retrieval"],
748	            rationale=(
749	                f"lossless opaque: deontic clause ({cue.family!r} cue "
750	                f"{cue.cue!r}) has no deterministic normalization — preserved "
751	                f"verbatim, surfaced as blocked_unsupported (never dropped)"
752	            ),
753	        )
754	        clause.term_ids.append(term.term_id)
755	        terms.append(term)
756	    return terms
757	
758	
759	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
760	# Completeness validator — every deontic constraint clause has a term
761	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
762	
763	def _clause_covered_by_terms(
764	    clause: Clause, term_spans: list[tuple[int, int, str]],
765	) -> Optional[str]:
766	    """Return a covering term_id if any contract term's span overlaps the clause."""
767	    for (s, e, tid) in term_spans:
768	        if s < clause.end and e > clause.start:
769	            return tid
770	    return None
771	
772	
773	def validate_completeness(
774	    clauses: list[Clause], contract: Any,
775	) -> list[ValidationError]:
776	    """COMPLETENESS invariant (the lossless gate): every deontic-marked constraint
777	    clause has a corresponding :class:`ContractTerm` (normalized OR opaque).
778	
779	    Keys off the PROMPT (the ledger), not the extractor output — this is the check
780	    ``validate_monotonicity`` structurally cannot make. A constraint-bearing clause
781	    with no covering term → ``clause_undispositioned`` (fatal interactive;
782	    autonomous preserves it as opaque and marks ``degraded_lossless``). Also flags
783	    ``clause_downgraded`` if a deontic clause's disposition was moved off
784	    ``explicit_constraint`` (the LLM tried to demote a hard constraint to context).
785	    """
786	    errors: list[ValidationError] = []
787	    # index every contract-term span (opaque terms included).
788	    term_spans: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []
789	    for term in contract.all_terms():
790	        for sp in term.spans:
791	            term_spans.append((sp.start, sp.end, term.term_id))
792	
793	    for clause in clauses:
794	        if not clause.is_constraint_bearing():
795	            continue
796	        # A deontic constraint clause must remain dispositioned as a constraint.
797	        if clause.disposition not in (DISP_EXPLICIT_CONSTRAINT, DISP_DELIVERABLE):
798	            errors.append(ValidationError(
799	                "clause_downgraded",
800	                f"deontic constraint clause {clause.clause_id!r} "
801	                f"({clause.deontic.cue!r}) was downgraded to "
802	                f"disposition={clause.disposition!r}",
803	                term_id=clause.clause_id,
804	            ))
805	        covering = _clause_covered_by_terms(clause, term_spans)
806	        if covering is None and not clause.term_ids:
807	            errors.append(ValidationError(
808	                "clause_undispositioned",
809	                f"deontic constraint clause {clause.clause_id!r} "
810	                f"(cue {clause.deontic.cue!r}, text {clause.text!r}) has no "
811	                f"corresponding contract term — a stated constraint would vanish",
812	                term_id=clause.clause_id,
813	            ))
814	    return errors
815

content.content:
def _canonicalize_source_type(
    value: Optional[str], alias_index: dict[str, str]
) -> tuple[Optional[str], bool]:
    """Map a source-type value to its ontology facet_id. Returns (canonical,
    mapped). Tries the value, its underscore/space variants, and a plural-stripped
    form. Unmapped → (value, False) so an unknown kind stays a first-class OPAQUE
    value, never dropped."""
    if not value:
        return value, False
    for cand in (
        value.strip().lower(),
        value.strip().replace("_", " ").lower(),
        value.strip().replace(" ", "_").lower(),
        value.strip().rstrip("s").lower(),
    ):
        if cand in alias_index:
            return alias_index[cand], True
    return value, False

def _stamp_ir(c: CandidateConstraint, *, opaque: bool = False) -> CandidateConstraint:
    """Fill a candidate's generic-IR fields from the registry (in place).

    The candidate's RAW dimension keys the registry; the row supplies the
    canonical subject/attribute/operator/stage_owner. ``normalization_status`` is
    ``exact`` when the registry recognized the dimension, else ``opaque`` — an
    opaque candidate is a FIRST-CLASS constraint we preserve verbatim, never drop
    (the monotonic-lossless core principle). Returns the same candidate.
    """
    row = _registry_row(c.dimension)
    if row is not None:
        c.subject = row.subject
        c.attribute = row.attribute
        # exclusion flips IN→NOT_IN already encoded in the row; a hard "only X"
        # keeps the row operator. A soft candidate stays PREFER-shaped only when
        # the row itself is PREFER; otherwise the operator reflects the relation
        # (force carries hard/soft separately).
        c.operator = row.operator
        c.stage_owner = row.stage_owner
        c.normalization_status = "opaque" if opaque else "exact"

Phase B (11b8fc3) inverted authority so DETERMINISTIC candidates can't be
dropped — but the monotonicity guarantee only covers constraints a deterministic
EXTRACTOR produces a candidate for. A stated constraint with no extractor (a bare
quality adjective, a source kind absent from the ontology, an exclusion of an
unknown kind) is INVISIBLE to validate_monotonicity: the survival set is the image
of the extractors, not the image of the prompt. When the LLM omits such a
constraint, nothing fails and it silently vanishes.

This lands Sol's lossless clause-ledger design (sol_situation_review §3), keyed off
the PROMPT rather than a cleverer extractor:

  * Lossless clause ledger (clause_ledger.py): deterministic segmentation of the
    whole prompt into stable clause/span IDs (quote-equality guaranteed), each with
    exactly one disposition.
  * Deontic-driven constraint detection: a clause carrying a modal/deontic cue
    (only / must / do not / no ___ / from YYYY onward / at least / a quality
    adjective) is DETERMINISTICALLY constraint-bearing and MUST yield a term. The
    LLM may refine but can never downgrade a deontic-marked clause. Domain-neutral:
    it recognizes the GRAMMAR of an instruction, so a never-seen source kind under
    an "only …" scope is still caught.
  * Generic deterministic parsers (lexicon/registry-driven, no per-task branch):
    quality (bare adjective → source.quality, hardness scope-inherited), negation/
    exclusion (strong prohibition verbs on any noun; weak "no X" only on a source
    noun → NOT_IN, never a positive query token), coordination ("A and B" → IN
    {A,B} sharing a boolean_group), and date-hardness inheritance ("only … from
    YYYY onward" → date GTE, hard). Each emits an ordinary CandidateConstraint so
    the existing deterministic-authoritative merge authors it.
  * OPAQUE preservation: a deontic-marked clause no term span covers becomes a
    first-class opaque ContractTerm (origin=explicit, force from its cue,
    normalization_status=opaque → blocked_unsupported) — preserved + disclosed,
    never silent. This is the lossless guarantee that does not depend on an
    extractor existing.
  * Completeness validator: every deontic constraint clause must have a
    corresponding term (normalized OR opaque) — clause_undispositioned /
    clause_downgraded. Keys off the prompt, the check candidate-only monotonicity
    cannot make. Computed into a separate list so the autonomous re-validation
    after the disclosure sweep cannot silently drop the ledger invariants.
  * The clause ledger is persisted on the artifact and emitted into to_dict ONLY
    when non-empty.

Fully behind default-OFF PG_GATE: OFF → no segmentation, no ledger candidates, no
opaque terms, no ledger invariants, and the artifact serializes to the legacy shape
(byte-identical to champion). The champion intake extractor and the frozen
faithfulness code are untouched — the new parsers augment reconcile_candidates
additively. 67 new/updated tests green; full planning suite 114 passed / 2 xfailed.
